titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Board of Research Associates in American Economic History Papers
Board of Research Associates in American Economic History Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 11, 2020 at 08:25

did.unittitle: Board of Research Associates in American Economic History papers
did.abstract: Correspondence, reports, studies, minutes of meetings, circular letters, financial reports, outlines of proposed surveys, annual reports, departmental reports, and monographs relative to the established purpose of the Board, which was sponsored by the Carnegie Institution of Washington, Department of Economics and Sociology. Included are interviews (ca. 1906-1907) with Southern farmers, professionals, government officials, and merchants on the economics of agriculture in the South. Also a number of interviews with blacks connected with the Tuskegee Institute, an insurance agent, a banker and laborers. Topics covered in the collection include labor legislation in Indiana, 1908; laws regarding seamen; immigration; labor problems in individual states; public poor relief in Wisconsin; trade unions; school systems; child labor laws; and mining laws.

corpname.part: Board of Research Associates in American Economic History

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Board of Research Associates in American Economic History Papers (MS 88). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Board of Research Associates in American Economic History, 1934.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in three parts: corrrespondence, monographs, and account book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The correspondence in this collection, arranged by the secretary of the Board of Research Associates, consists of 16 boxes. The entire collection consists of 23 boxes. These papers were arranged by Miss Vera Timm, Secretary of the Board of Research Associates, and were turned over to Yale Library. The Board in 1934 had voted to give the papers to Yale Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

corpname.part: Board of Research Associates in American Economic History

did.unittitle: Correspondence
Letters, reports, and minutes of meetings, 1907-1916, and outlines of books, 1905-1930
Letters and papers, ca. 1906-1928, concerning the publication of books by the Carnegie Institution and the Board of Research Associates
Letters, reports, and syllabi of books written for the Board of Research Associates, ca. 1906-1927
Letters & papers, ca. 1906-1916, including letters from Prof. Farnam & Pres. Butterfield, financial statements, reports, and outlines concerning surveys financed by the Carnegie Institution.
Annual reports, financial reports, departmental reports, and outlines and bills, 1912-1916, of the Carnegie Institution, department of economics and sociology.

Financial reports, checks, bank statements, and papers, 1917-1933, of the Board of Research Associates, including letters and papers, 1916, relative to the separation from Carnegie Institution of Washington.
Letters and papers, ca. 1928-1933, concerning books published by the Board of Research Associates: History of Southern Agriculture before 1860
Letters and papers ca. 1911-1917 concerning the publication of books and monographs on agriculture
Essay on homestead exemptions, 1910, by Prof. Farnam, and letters by and to Miss. Vera A. Timm ca. 1936-1940 concerning papers of Prof. Farnam.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: The monographs in this collection, consisting of 31 studies under the direction of Prof, Henry W. Farnam, are catalogued individually under authors. List of authors follows.

did.unittitle: Monographs

odd.head: General
odd.p: The monographs in this collection, consisting of 31 studies under the direction of Prof, Henry W. Farnam, are catalogued individually under authors. List of authors follows.

did.unittitle: Austin, Charles Burgess: Labor Legislation in Indiana , 1908 251 l. typewritten. 2 folders
Badger, Ralph Eastman: Report Giving Receipts and Expenditures Connected with the Public Lands Service of the United States, 1888-1913 (1916). 9l. typewritten. 1 folder
Baldwin, Foy Spencer, 1870- : Recent Massachusetts Labor Legislation, 1901-1908 (1908). typed. 1 folder
Bancroft, Everett Clair: Notes on Laws Regarding Seamen, Immigration, Paupers, etc. , 1916.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: California, 2 bundles; Connecticut, 2 bundles; Delaware, 1 bundle; Florida, 1 bundle; Ga., 1 bundle; Maine, 1 bundle; Massachusetts, 2 bundles; Maryland, 1 bundle; New Hampshire, 1 bundle; New Jersey, 1 bundle; New York, 2 bundles; North Carolina, l bundle; Rhode Island, 1 bundle; South Carolina 1 bundle; Virginia, 1 bundle; Chinese and U.S., 1 bundle; U.S. Congressional Laws. Immigration, 1798-1913, 1 bundle. 21 bundles. 2 folders.

did.unittitle: Barnes, Charles Brinton, 1870- : The Longshoremen , (New York, 1915). Corrected proof sheets of a study published by the Russell Sage foundation.

Bedford, Scott Elias William, 1876- : The Labor Legislation of Kansas , 1909. 330l. typewritten 3 folders. Filed with this are three letters to Bedford from: Frank Gilday, 1911 Mar 31; Thomas Morrissey, 1911 Jun 13 and Leon Besson, 1911 Sep 27
Coulter, John Lee, 1881- : The Public Land Policy of Dakota Territory and North and South Dakota , 1909. 145l. typewritten. 1 folder. Completed 1909 Jun 10.
Crafer, Thomas Woodside Bentley

The Administration of Public Poor Relief in Wisconsin . Part I of a monograph for the Carnegie institution of Wash. D.C. 1907. 106l. typewritten. 1 folder; Part II of a monograph for the Carnegie Institution of Wash. D.C. 1907. 112l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Trade Unions and the Law in Wisconsin , 1909.160l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Diamond, Herbert Maynard, 1892- : Federal Children's Bureau , 1915. 14l. typewritten. 1 folder

Downey, Ezekiel Henry, 1879-1922: History of Labor Legislation on Iowa , 1910. Published, Iowa Economic History Series, State Historical Society of Iowa. Edited by Benjamin F. Shambaugh, Iowa City, 1910. 394l. typewritten. 2 folders.
Dozier, Howard Douglass: A Brief History of the Public School System of Tennessee , 1916. 20l typewritten. 1 folder.

Edward, Alba M, 1872-1947: Federal Legislation for the Protection of American Seamen . New Haven, Conn., 1907. 2 bundles of notes. The Legislation of the New England States Governing the Descent and Distribution of Property . 1907. 292l manuscript. 1 folder. The Maritime Hospital Service of the United States . New Haven, 1907.114l+ tp. and table of contents. 1 notebook.

Field, Arthur Sargent, 1874-1932: The Child Labor and Factory Law of New Jersey (A thesis submitted to the faculty of social science in Yale University for part of the requirements for the degree of Doctor of Philosophy, 1909 May 1) Published American Economic Association, 3d series, xi, no. 3, Cambridge, Mass. pp.229, 1910 under title, "The Child Labor Policy of New Jersey." 313l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Hagerty, James Edward: The History of the Poor Laws of Ohio, Indiana, & Illinois , 1908 by James E. Hagerty and Beatrice H. Sheets. 301l. typewritten. 2 volumes.
Isaacs, Nathan: The Mining Laws of Ohio, Indiana, and Illinois , 1908

McKitrick, Reuben: Syllabus of a Report to Carnegie Institution on the Legislative Disposition of Public Lands in Texas , 1908. 44l. typed. 1 folder. The Public Land System of Texas , 1911, published as "The public land system of Texas, 1823-1910", in bull. of the Univ. of Wisconsin, no. 905, economics and political science series, IX no. 1, pp. 1-172, Madison, Wis., 1918. Also reprinted in part in Farnam, Henry W., Chapters in the history of social legislation in the United States to 1860 , Washington, D.C. 1938, pp.140-147. 1971 typewritten. 1 folder.
Parry, Carl E.: Labor Legislation of Michigan , 1909 303l. typewritten. 2 folders.
Peacock, D.L.

Interviews with Southern men . Collected for social legislation of the southern states: Administration officials, bank officers, lawyers, and judges, men connected with educational institutions, colored people, cotton dealers, and merchants, plantation owners and superintendents, miscellaneous interviews. Material collected in 1906 & 1907. 127l typewritten. 1 folder.
Laws of the Reconstruction Period Relating to Labor , circa 1910. 117l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Mining Legislation in the Southern States , 1909. 263l. MS. 1 folder

Riley, Thomas James, 1870- : A History of the Poor Law and of Poor Relief in Missouri, Being an Account of the Legislative and Institutional Development of the State's Charities , 1909. 70l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Sheets, Beatrice H : A History of the Poor Laws of Ohio , 1909 by James E. Hagerty and Beatrice H. Sheets. 110l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Steele, H. Wirt: Labor Legislation in Maryland ,1908. 211l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Towles, John Ker, 1880- : Factory Legislation in Rhode Island , 1908 155l. typewritten. 1 volume.
Underwood, J. Harding: Homesteads and Exemptions , 1909. 170l. typewritten. 1 folder.
Carnegie Institution of Washington Department of Economics and Sociology Account book, 1907-1908 (Filed alone at end of collection)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Laid in is Examination of accounts, 1917-1933 by Petze and Schuyler.
